name: Coquille St.jacques Savoie
recipeIngredient: 1 Onion, halved and stuck with a clove
recipeInstructions:
Bouquet Garni - In a piece of cheesecloth, tie the following: 3 to 4 sprigs of parsley, 1 bay leaf, 1/2 celery stalk with leaves, 1/2 tsp dry thyme. Leave the string long enough to hang the garni in the pot and tie the string to the pot handle for easy removal. In a large saucepan combine the wine, onion, Bouquet Garni and peppercorns. Season with salt and cover. Simmer over medium heat for 20 minutes. Add the scallops. Cover and simmer for an additional 5 minutes. Remove the pan from the heat. Drain the scallops and keep them warm.
1 In a bowl beat the Dijon mustard, Dry English mustard, tomato paste and lemon juice into a smooth paste. Add the egg yolks to the mustard mixture and beat until it is light and creamy.
2 Melt the butter in the top part of a double boiler over simmering water. The butter should be warm, not hot. Keep the water simmering. Slowly whisk the yolk mixture into the melted butter. The sauce should get thick and creamy. Do not let it come to a boil or the yolks will curdle. Remove the sauce from the heat. Season with salt and pepper.
3 Add the parsley, capers and warm scallops to the sauce. Serve immediately, garnished with chives and lemon quarters. Note: In certain regions of France this course is served surrounded by boiled potato balls and buttered carrots.
